title: stapelia variegata var. clypeata
intro: Stapelia variegata var. clypeata, commonly known as the 'Cape Clawless Cactus', is a rare and unique succulent plant species that belongs to the Asclepiadaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include the presence of ergine, a precursor to the ergot alkaloids. The health benefits associated with Stapelia variegata var. clypeata include its potential use in treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant's extract or resin is often used to create tonics and remedies, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human use.
